name: HTTP Router with Middleware
description: Implement an HTTP router with path parameter extraction and middleware chain support across two modules. Module route_types.py must define: Request dataclass with fields method (str), path (str), headers (dict, default empty), body (any, default None), params (dict, default empty); Response dataclass with fields status_code (int), body (any), headers (dict, default empty); Route dataclass with fields method (str), pattern (str), handler (callable); Middleware as an abstract base class with abstract method process(request, next_handler) -> Response. Module router.py must import from route_types.py and implement Router class with methods: add_route(method, pattern, handler) supporting path params like /users/:id, use(middleware) to add middleware, handle(request) -> Response that matches routes, extracts path params into request.params, runs middleware chain, and returns 404 Response if no route matches. Middleware executes in the order added, each calling next_handler to continue the chain.
test_imports: from route_types import Request, Response, Route, Middleware
signature:
# route_types.py
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Callable
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

@dataclass
class Request:
    method: str
    path: str
    headers: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
    body: Any = None
    params: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)

@dataclass
class Response:
    status_code: int
    body: Any = None
    headers: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)

@dataclass
class Route:
    method: str
    pattern: str
    handler: Callable

class Middleware(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def process(self, request: Request, next_handler: Callable) -> Response: ...

# router.py
from route_types import Request, Response, Middleware

class Router:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def add_route(self, method: str, pattern: str, handler: Callable) -> None: ...
    def use(self, middleware: Middleware) -> None: ...
    def handle(self, request: Request) -> Response: ...
tests:
def test_basic_route():
    router = Router()
    router.add_route('GET', '/hello', lambda req: Response(200, 'Hello World'))
    resp = router.handle(Request('GET', '/hello'))
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.body == 'Hello World'

def test_path_params():
    router = Router()
    router.add_route('GET', '/users/:id', lambda req: Response(200, f"User {req.params['id']}"))
    resp = router.handle(Request('GET', '/users/42'))
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.body == 'User 42'

def test_404_not_found():
    router = Router()
    router.add_route('GET', '/exists', lambda req: Response(200, 'ok'))
    resp = router.handle(Request('GET', '/not-here'))
    assert resp.status_code == 404

def test_method_matching():
    router = Router()
    router.add_route('GET', '/item', lambda req: Response(200, 'get'))
    router.add_route('POST', '/item', lambda req: Response(201, 'post'))
    assert router.handle(Request('GET', '/item')).body == 'get'
    assert router.handle(Request('POST', '/item')).body == 'post'

def test_middleware_execution():
    router = Router()
    log = []
    class LogMiddleware(Middleware):
        def process(self, request, next_handler):
            log.append('before')
            resp = next_handler(request)
            log.append('after')
            return resp
    router.use(LogMiddleware())
    router.add_route('GET', '/test', lambda req: Response(200, 'ok'))
    router.handle(Request('GET', '/test'))
    assert log == ['before', 'after']

def test_middleware_order():
    router = Router()
    order = []
    class MW1(Middleware):
        def process(self, request, next_handler):
            order.append('mw1_before')
            resp = next_handler(request)
            order.append('mw1_after')
            return resp
    class MW2(Middleware):
        def process(self, request, next_handler):
            order.append('mw2_before')
            resp = next_handler(request)
            order.append('mw2_after')
            return resp
    router.use(MW1())
    router.use(MW2())
    router.add_route('GET', '/x', lambda req: Response(200, 'ok'))
    router.handle(Request('GET', '/x'))
    assert order == ['mw1_before', 'mw2_before', 'mw2_after', 'mw1_after']
constraints: Middleware process() receives request and next_handler callable
Middleware chain executes in order added, wrapping like an onion
Unmatched routes return Response with status_code 404
route_types.py exports Request, Response, Route, Middleware
router.py imports from route_types.py and exports Router
